title: Residential Wiring Rough-In Simulator
summary: Learners route branch-circuit wiring through a residential mock-up, placing boxes, switches, receptacles, and lighting outlets while keeping conductor identification and bonding rules in view.
learningObjectives: Recognize common residential branch-circuit devices and cable paths.
Place receptacles, switches, and lighting outlets in a realistic rough-in layout.
Apply grounding and bonding concepts to a basic dwelling installation.

environment.setting: Open-framed house model with rooms, stud bays, boxes, cable reels, and branch circuits
environment.focus: Basic layout interpretation and installation planning

interactables.label: Box placement kit
interactables.teaches: Shows where switches, receptacles, and fixture boxes belong in the plan.

interactables.label: Cable routing overlay
interactables.teaches: Demonstrates practical paths for branch-circuit cable runs.

interactables.label: Conductor ID legend
interactables.teaches: Reinforces hot, neutral, and bonding conductor identification.

interactables.label: Room energize check
interactables.teaches: Confirms whether the placed wiring supports the intended device operation.

tasks: Place boxes for a bedroom lighting and receptacle circuit.
Route cable from the panel to each device without missing a required connection.
Assign hot, neutral, and bonding conductors correctly.
Energize the mock-up and verify switch-controlled lighting behavior.
completion: Learner completes a functional branch-circuit rough-in.
Learner verifies device operation at the end of the installation.
